title: ferray-core: no empty/empty_like constructors (uninitialized arrays)
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's np.empty() and np.empty_like() allocate arrays without initializing their contents, which is faster than zeros() when you're about to overwrite every element anyway. ferray-core's creation module has zeros(), ones(), from_elem(), from_vec() — all require initialization. For performance-critical paths (ufunc output buffers, temporary workspace), an uninit constructor using MaybeUninit<T> would avoid redundant memsets. This needs careful unsafe code but is a meaningful optimization for large arrays.
